detailed_analysis: shared_keyed_map_formats.py is a small (78-line), well-named pluggable registry that maps a format name ('json'/'toml') to a frozen SharedKeyedMapFormat dataclass holding an extension plus a deserialize/serialize callable pair. It is consumed by shared_keyed_map_io.py via get_format(layout.file_format). The code is clean and intention-revealing: get_format implements proper fail-fast (descriptive KeyError listing known formats), the dataclass is frozen, and the imported json_format/toml_format modules expose matching deserialize(text)->MutableMapping and serialize(Mapping)->str signatures. There are no security smells in this module itself (no secrets, no injection, no unsafe deserialization here; the SafeLoader/secret-policy concerns live in the format handlers and the I/O gateway respectively). The two genuine code-health concerns are structural, not behavioural: (1) the module keeps a module-level mutable dict _FORMAT_REGISTRY mutated by register_format (M7 global mutable state), and (2) the two register_format(...) calls at module scope (lines 60-77) execute on import, making import non-idempotent and the populated registry an import-order side effect (W5 import side effects). register_format is also documented to silently overwrite an existing entry (last-writer-wins), which is a deliberate design choice but trades away fail-fast on accidental double-registration. Spec-wise, the module is functional but is entirely absent from architecture.md's module map (§4) and traceability table (§9), and from the cross-cutting-invariant treatment — a governance/traceability gap given §9's claim that every module's requirement coverage is recorded.

findings.problem: A top-level mutable dict (_FORMAT_REGISTRY) is mutated by register_format and read by get_format. This is module-global mutable state: all callers and all importers share one hidden, order-dependent registry instance. Re-registration is silent last-writer-wins (lines 43-45 docstring), so an accidental duplicate or import-order collision overwrites a handler with no error — a quiet coupling point and a fail-fast gap.

Two register_format(...) statements run at module import time, mutating global state as a side effect of `import`. The registry is therefore populated implicitly by import order rather than by an explicit, testable initialisation call. This makes the import non-idempotent in spirit (re-import is fine here only because the dict assignment is idempotent), couples correctness to whether this module is imported before any get_format call, and complicates isolated testing of the empty-registry / KeyError path.

findings.evidence:
def register_format(name: str, format_handler: SharedKeyedMapFormat) -> None:
    """Register a handler under ``name``. Re-registration replaces the
    prior entry (last writer wins) ..."""
    _FORMAT_REGISTRY[name] = format_handler
findings.problem: register_format performs no validation: it does not check that `name` is non-empty, does not warn on overwriting an existing key, and accepts any object as format_handler without asserting the callable contract. AGENTS.md §8 (Fail Fast, validate at boundaries) favours an explicit guard or at least a debug log on silent replacement. Low severity because the documented contract is deliberate; flagged as a boundary-validation hint.

deviations.description: shared_keyed_map_formats.py (and the whole formats/ package + shared_keyed_map_io.py) is absent from the §4 module map table. The map claims '19 modules, 2 852 lines' and assigns every module a layer and inward-dependency list; this Layer-3-style format registry/port has no row, so the architecture document is stale relative to the v0.5 MCP-server shared keyed-map code it governs.

§9 asserts 'Every requirement ... has at least one module carrying it' and 'Any future requirement that has no carrying module is a defect.' shared_keyed_map_formats.py carries the format-handler seam that FR-09 (mcp_server matrix) and the shared keyed-map render/discovery paths depend on, yet it appears nowhere in the traceability table and has no listed verifying test, leaving the FR-09 / shared-keyed-map mapping undocumented.

verdict_summary: Clean, well-named 78-line format registry; only structural smell is a module-level mutable registry populated by import-time side effects (M7/W5). No security or correctness defects. Spec gap: module is undocumented in architecture §4/§9.
